title: GJMS weights and abstract operators
summary: Defines the critical weights wₖ = k − n/2 and wₖ′ = −k − n/2, then packages weighted tangential operators and their low-order family notation.

mathematicalContent.heading: Critical GJMS weights
mathematicalContent.body: For the operator of order 2k on an n-dimensional conformal manifold, the ambient extension has input homogeneity k−n/2. Applying the ambient Laplacian k times lowers homogeneity by 2k, producing output weight −k−n/2.

mathematicalContent.heading: Abstract operator interface
mathematicalContent.body: An AbstractOperator does not construct the GJMS operator. It stores a weighted ambient operator already known to preserve the required homogeneities and to be tangential at wₖ.

mathematicalContent.heading: Families and low orders
mathematicalContent.body: A Family chooses one such abstract operator for every k and certifies that the stored index is k. The names P0, P2, and P4 select k=0,1,2; they do not identify these operators with explicit curvature formulas.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the critical input homogeneity for the kth ambient construction on an n-dimensional conformal manifold.
Defines the homogeneity after k applications of the ambient Laplacian.
Proves the expected weight drop: each ambient Laplacian lowers homogeneity by two, so k applications lower it by 2k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_eq_inputWeight_sub_two_mul (n : ℝ) (k : ℕ) : outputWeight n k = inputWeight n k - 2 * (k : ℝ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluates the critical input weight at k=0.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputWeight_zero (n : ℝ) : inputWeight n 0 = - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluates the output weight at k=0; it agrees with the input weight because no Laplacian is applied.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_zero (n : ℝ) : outputWeight n 0 = - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluates the critical input weight for the second-order operator.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputWeight_one (n : ℝ) : inputWeight n 1 = 1 - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluates the output weight for the second-order operator.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_one (n : ℝ) : outputWeight n 1 = -1 - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluates the critical input weight for the fourth-order operator.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputWeight_two (n : ℝ) : inputWeight n 2 = 2 - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Evaluates the output weight for the fourth-order operator.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeight_two (n : ℝ) : outputWeight n 2 = -2 - n / 2

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Specializes the general input-weight formula to the dimension n stored in the fixed ambient calculus.
Specializes the general output-weight formula to the dimension stored in the fixed ambient calculus.
Restates the 2k weight drop for the fixed ambient calculus.
correspondence.leanType: theorem calculusOutputWeight_eq_calculusInputWeight_sub_two_mul (k : ℕ) : calculusOutputWeight CalConf k = calculusInputWeight CalConf k - 2 * (k : ℝ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Computes the fixed-calculus input weight at k=0.
Computes the fixed-calculus output weight at k=0.
Computes the fixed-calculus input weight at k=1.
Computes the fixed-calculus output weight at k=1.
Computes the fixed-calculus input weight at k=2.
Computes the fixed-calculus output weight at k=2.

Packages an index k together with a weighted tangential ambient operator whose stored input and output weights are exactly the two GJMS weights. Tangentiality and weight preservation come from the stored operator; they are not proved by this structure.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: P_{2k}:\mathcal E[w_k]\longrightarrow\mathcal E[w'_k],\qquad w_k=k-\frac n2,\quad w'_k=-k-\frac n2.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Defines the expected differential order from the stored index k.
Returns the input weight stored in the underlying weighted tangential operator.
Returns the output weight stored in the underlying weighted tangential operator.
Forgets the weights and tangentiality certificate and returns the underlying ambient operator.
correspondence.leanType: def toOperator (P : AbstractOperator CalConf) : Operator (Conf := Conf)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Extracts the stored assertion that the underlying operator sends homogeneous functions of the input weight to homogeneous functions of the output weight.
correspondence.leanType: theorem mapsWeight (P : AbstractOperator CalConf) : CalConf.MapsHomogeneousWeight P.toOperator P.inputWeightOf P.outputWeightOf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Extracts the stored tangentiality assertion: on homogeneous extensions of the input weight, equal boundary values give equal boundary values after applying P.
correspondence.mathematicalFormula: f\equiv g\pmod Q\quad\Longrightarrow\quad Pf\equiv Pg\pmod Q.
correspondence.leanType: theorem tangential (P : AbstractOperator CalConf) : CalConf.IsTangentialAtWeight P.toOperator P.inputWeightOf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Identifies the stored input weight with the critical GJMS value determined by P.k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem inputWeightOf_eq (P : AbstractOperator CalConf) : P.inputWeightOf = calculusInputWeight CalConf P.k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Identifies the stored output weight with the GJMS output value determined by P.k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeightOf_eq (P : AbstractOperator CalConf) : P.outputWeightOf = calculusOutputWeight CalConf P.k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Combines the two stored weight identities to show that an abstract kth operator lowers homogeneity by 2k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem outputWeightOf_eq_inputWeightOf_sub_two_mul (P : AbstractOperator CalConf) : P.outputWeightOf = P.inputWeightOf - 2 * (P.k : ℝ)

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Packages one AbstractOperator for every natural number k and a proof that the kth chosen operator really stores the index k.
Selects the kth abstract operator from a family.
correspondence.leanType: def get (P : Family CalConf) (k : ℕ) : AbstractOperator CalConf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves that the operator selected at k has stored index k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem get_k (P : Family CalConf) (k : ℕ) : (P.get k).k = k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Consequently, the kth member of a family has expected order 2k.
correspondence.leanType: theorem get_order (P : Family CalConf) (k : ℕ) : (P.get k).order = 2 * k

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Names the family member with k=0.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev P0 {CalConf : Calculus Conf} (P : Family CalConf) : AbstractOperator CalConf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Names the family member with k=1, hence expected order two.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev P2 {CalConf : Calculus Conf} (P : Family CalConf) : AbstractOperator CalConf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Names the family member with k=2, hence expected order four.
correspondence.leanType: abbrev P4 {CalConf : Calculus Conf} (P : Family CalConf) : AbstractOperator CalConf

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: The operator P0 stores index zero.
The operator P2 stores index one.
The operator P4 stores index two.
The expected order of P0 is zero.
The expected order of P2 is two.
The expected order of P4 is four.

references.note: Original construction of conformally invariant powers of the Laplacian.

references.locator: Ambient Laplacian and homogeneous extension discussion
references.note: Ambient-metric background for the formal construction.

limitations: The numerical weight identities are fully proved. AbstractOperator and Family are interfaces: they store weight preservation and tangentiality rather than constructing them. This file does not prove that an ambient Laplacian power is tangential or identify P2 and P4 with explicit conformal Laplacian and Paneitz formulas.
